title: narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. radinganorum
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. radinganorum, also known as the wild daffodil, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating conditions such as fever, colds, and respiratory issues. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing stress, promoting digestion, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to have a range of therapeutic benefits.
